req.title: System-Admin creates payroll-run
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to create a payroll-run through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path creation by an authenticated admin (EP – valid partition), (2) missing mandatory fields returning 400 (NEG – invalid input), (3) boundary on the payroll-period name at its maximum allowed length (BVA), (4) unauthenticated access returning 401 (NEG – invalid authentication state, heuristic corrected from STC to NEG because the test exercises a negative/error pathway due to absent credentials, not a state-transition sequence), and (5) forbidden role returning 403 (NEG / DT – role decision column). The violation was test[3] being marked negative kind but assigned heuristic STC; STC is reserved for positive state-transition coverage. The fix reassigns test[3] heuristic to NEG, which is consistent with its negative kind and the error pathway it covers.

tests.name: test_admin_creates_payroll_run_success
preconditions: No existing payroll-run for the same period
actions: POST /payroll-runs with all mandatory fields populated and valid admin auth token
Query audit log for the returned entity id
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
Audit record exists for the created payroll-run id
trace: R-WEB-037 – positive outcome: payroll-run created, audit record written, 200/201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_admin_creates_payroll_run_success(admin_auth_token, api_client, audit_log_client, unique_period):
    payload = {
        "period_name": unique_period,
        "start_date": "2024-06-01",
        "end_date": "2024-06-30",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/payroll-runs",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Unexpected status: {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    entity_id = body.get("id")
    assert entity_id, "Response must contain a non-empty entity id"
    audit_records = audit_log_client.fetch(entity_id=entity_id, action="CREATE")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "Audit record must be written on successful creation"

tests.name: test_create_payroll_run_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400
actions: POST /payroll-runs with mandatory fields omitted (empty payload) and valid admin auth token
Capture response status and error body
expected: Response body contains field-level validation errors
trace: R-WEB-037 – error pathway: 400 on validation error when mandatory fields are absent

tests.name: test_create_payroll_run_period_name_at_max_length
preconditions: Maximum allowed length for period_name is known (e.g. 100 characters)
actions: POST /payroll-runs with period_name exactly at the maximum allowed length (100 chars) and all other mandatory fields valid
expected: Response body contains a non-empty entity id
trace: R-WEB-037 – boundary: period_name at max-length boundary must succeed; one character over must fail (max+1 not tested here)
tests.executable:
import pytest

MAX_PERIOD_NAME_LEN = 100

def test_create_payroll_run_period_name_at_max_length(admin_auth_token, api_client, unique_suffix):
    period_name = ("P" * (MAX_PERIOD_NAME_LEN - len(unique_suffix))) + unique_suffix
    assert len(period_name) == MAX_PERIOD_NAME_LEN
    payload = {
        "period_name": period_name,
        "start_date": "2024-07-01",
        "end_date": "2024-07-31",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post(
        "/payroll-runs",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {admin_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Max-length period_name should be accepted, got {resp.status_code}"
    assert resp.json().get("id"), "Response must include entity id"

tests.name: test_create_payroll_run_unauthenticated_returns_401
preconditions: No authentication token is provided (unauthenticated state)
actions: POST /payroll-runs with a fully valid payload but no Authorization header
trace: R-WEB-037 – error pathway: 401 if unauthenticated; NEG covers the invalid-credential partition where no auth token is supplied

tests.name: test_create_payroll_run_non_admin_role_returns_403
actions: POST /payroll-runs with a fully valid payload using a non-admin auth token
trace: R-WEB-037 – error pathway: 403 if role is forbidden; DT column: authenticated=true, role=non-admin -> forbidden
